title: Fix race condition: make task tier classification synchronous at creation
context:
In app/routers/tasks.py, create_task() currently classifies model_tier using asyncio.create_task() (background). This means the orchestrator can pick up the task before model_tier is set. All other work (task_tier.py service, ModelChooser short-circuit, workflow session_refs) is already done.

Fix: move tier classification to be synchronous (inline await) in create_task(), before the response is returned.

In create_task() in app/routers/tasks.py:
1. Remove the _classify_and_update() inner function and its asyncio.create_task() call
2. After 'await db.flush()' and 'await db.refresh(db_task)', add:
   if not db_task.model_tier:
       tier = await classify_task_tier(db_task, db)
       db_task.model_tier = tier
       await db.commit()
       await db.refresh(db_task)
3. Build task_result AFTER the classification, so the response includes the tier

The classify_task_tier function already has a 2s timeout and safe fallback to 'standard'. Import already exists at the top of tasks.py.

Note: db.commit() inside create_task may conflict with the outer commit. Check if tasks.py uses an explicit commit at the end or relies on middleware. If it uses middleware commit, just set db_task.model_tier = tier without calling db.commit() — the middleware will commit it with the rest of the transaction.
acceptance:
1. POST /api/tasks with no model_tier -> task in response has model_tier set (not null)
2. POST /api/tasks with model_tier='strong' -> task keeps 'strong', not overwritten
3. When LM Studio is unreachable, task creation still succeeds within 3s with model_tier='standard'
4. Task creation response includes the classified model_tier field
5. No asyncio.create_task background calls remain for tier classification
6. Existing task creation tests still pass
